[[File:StaticOver.gif|2000px]]
{{Underline|90d5ff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance.png|quote-text=
The tornadoes returned once more.
Stronger, and fiercer than before.
They chased me—
And I ran, slipping just beyond their reach. {{Underline|90d5ff}} I searched for the shore
The one I saw in daylight.
But no matter how I looked, I could not reach it.
Yesterday morning, it was so close.
I swam with all I had,
But the shore moved away,
As if it were alive. {{Underline|90d5ff}} One tornado caught hold of me,
But morning sunshine cracked through.
I watched as houses began to form
In mid-air,
Floating like gravity meant nothing at all. {{Underline|90d5ff}}
Now, I am somewhere
That water and night refuse to touch.
This is no normal place.
If anyone reads this…
I am likely already gone;
starved to death.
|person=UNKNOWN}}
{{Underline|90d5ff}} {{Blurquote|size=7000x7000px|image=Land's Avoidance bg.png|quote-text=
{{Underline|fff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance 5.png|quote-text=
{{Class|p1=4|p2=5|p3=5}}
}}
{{Underline|fff}}
[[Level 966]], known as '''"Land's Avoidance"''', is the 967th level of [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]].
{{Underline|fff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance 5.png|quote-text=
« Description »
}}
{{Underline|fff}} {{Blurimage|file=Land's Avoidance 3.png|caption=
Are The Lands Real?}} [[Level 966]] appears as a vast, and theoretically boundless ocean that expands endlessly in every direction. No natural landmasses; such as islands, coastlines, or underwater plateaus, have ever been documented within the level. The horizon is distant and featureless, and attempts to sail or swim toward it make no change in scenery. Despite its tranquil surface, the level hides unpredictable phenomena. Individuals usually describe the overwhelming sensation of isolation. The water of [[Level 966]] unusually feels calm despite chaos the level offer. Its color ranges from a pale blue to a dark, almost ink-like color depending on the time of day; although there is no major celestial bodies; such as sun or moon, to mark the passage of time. Beneath the surface a black void exists, with no visible seafloor or marine life. Even with advanced diving equipment, visibility drains after a few meters, and sonar signals fail to bounce back. Occasionally, subtly seen structures; such as submerged rooftops, lamp posts, or entire stairways, can be seen far below, situated in the water as if falling forever. These sights, however, are fleeting and usually disappear when approached. Sounds on [[Level 966]] behave unnaturally. Echo of voices sound stretch or not heard at all, and distant splashes may be heard with no visible cause. Some individuals report hearing subtle music randomly appearing across the waves; old piano melodies, distorted radio static, or lullabies that stir forgotten memories. These auditory phenomena usually lead individuals off-course. Compass needles spin randomly, and GPS devices fail entirely, which make navigation impossible. {{Blurimage|align=left|file=Land's Avoidance 4.png|caption=
The Night Awaits}} Survival in [[Level 966]] is a delicate balance between drifting and resisting the urge to chase illusions. Occasionally, strange objects appear floating on the water; old suitcases, framed photographs, juvenile toys, and doorframes that lead to [[The Void]]. These items usually have signs of prolonged water damage, but they feel dry when touched. Despite some objects are harmless, other objects have been reported to induce heat, cold, or a subtle hum when held. Food and fresh water are entirely absent, and attempts to bring provisions from other levels usually end with them mysteriously disappearing or spoiling instantly. Prolonged staying in the environment may lead to fatigue, disorientation, and in rare cases, vivid hallucinations of nonexistent islands or companions. Those who have stayed the longest focus less about escape; and more about listening to the waves of the ocean. During the time of dawn and evening, strange structures begin to form in the sky above; houses, hovering hundreds of feet in the air. They appear new, usually resembling suburban homes, cabins, or fragments of an apartment, with visible details like lights flickering in the windows, and opened entrance doors. Some float still, but some other rotate slowly or change positions. Attempts to reach them; whether by drone, flare, or climbing through levitating debris, have all ended in failure or disappearance. By noon or nightfall, these homes bleed into non-existence. {{Underline|fff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance 5.png|quote-text=
« Lethal Hazards »
}}
{{Underline|fff}}
« Tornadoes »
{{Underline|fff}} {{Blurimage|align=center|file=Land's Avoidance 2.png|caption=
Growing Dread
}} Tornadoes in [[Level 966]] do not follow [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]] logic. They form without warning. They spiral down rapidly across the ocean and defying physics by forming over calm water with no windy environment. These cyclones can remain stationary or move in purposeful patterns; some tornadoes even appear to chase individuals over long distances. Unlike regular tornadoes, these tornadoes emit no roaring winds; instead, they produce low, and guttural sounds like distant groaning or reversed speech. Boats caught by their force are lifted into the air, and bended apart mid-rotation, or quietly erased from view. Witnesses describe that the tornadoes seem to "decide" who to chase.
« Thunderstorms »
{{Underline|fff}} Thunderstorms arrive like slow giants, who roll in from unpredictable directions. The sky during night becomes black, the stars snuffed out entirely, and a heavy, and humid pressure comes in. Flashes of lightning effectively illuminate the sky and the clouds. Thunder does not crackle; instead, it rumbles from below the surface of the water, and sometimes shaking boats or making individuals unconscious. These storms usually last for hours, and during their presence, electronic equipment either malfunctions to the point of no return or transmits garbled human voices crying for help in unknown languages.
« Tsunamis »
{{Underline|fff}} Tsunamis in [[Level 966]] are rare but catastrophic. They do not build gradually; instead, they rise from still water with no apparent cause. When a tsunami strikes, there is no preceding earthquake or warning current; the water simply lifts. Some witnesses have seen structures grabbed by the waves: staircases, pieces of broken houses, or towers. These massive waves seem to target specific locations, and curiously, many disappear without reaching the sea surface. Witnesses usually describe a sudden change in pressure and a tense sense of dread moments before one appears.
« Lightning »
{{Underline|fff}} Lightning in this level behaves like an entity than a force of nature. Bolts split the sky in unnatural colors; crimson, violet, deep green, that strike the sea in silence or with inhuman screeches. Occasionally, the lightning freezes mid-strike, and hovers above the ocean for several seconds before disappearing. Strikes do not always cause damage, but the unfortunate individuals hit by them are usually left changed: disoriented, unable to speak, or plagued by impossible memories. Some witnesses report having seen faces or blinking eyes inside the flashes. Other witnesses claim that the lightning leaves behind small, and glowing orbs that sink through the water, then disappear. {{Underline|fff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance 5.png|quote-text=
« Entities »
}}
{{Underline|fff}} [[Level 966]] is known to frequently have [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]] and cases of the [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. The level's relentless isolation, with its lack of food, shelter, and warmth, usually leads to mental and physical deterioration, which push individuals toward either infection or transformation. {{Underline|fff}}
{{Blurquote|image=Land's Avoidance 5.png|quote-text=
« Entrances & Exits »
}}
{{Underline|fff}}
« Entrances »
{{Underline|fff}} * Falling asleep on a bed or a raft composed of glass in [[Level 118]] may cause one to awaken floating in [[Level 966]]. * Contacting in a tensely reflective puddle in [[Level 917]] may result in a sudden transition to [[Level 966]]. * Using a mirror-framed painting of the ocean in [[Level 913]] may behave as a one-way portal to [[Level 966]].
« Exits »
{{Underline|fff}} * Swimming toward a shore that does not recede; if one manages to reach one of the rare solid shorelines that appears during dawn, it may lead to [[Level 984]] or [[Level 994]]. * Entering a floating house, with rarely reachable height, may transition one to [[Level 993]], or [[Level 989]]. * Getting pulled underwater by unseen forces, usually after prolonged amount of time in the same area, has a rare chance of leading to [[Level 969]], although it is usually fatal. ---- ---- |person='''Author''': [[User:Neverending Joke|Never]]
'''Greenlit by''': [[User:SherlsF|SherlsF]]
'''Images''': Image 2 is by a [https://pin.it/11bg1ZE2P Pinterest Image], heavily edited by [[User:Neverending Joke|Never]]. Image 3 is by a [https://pin.it/SUEnCR5BW Pinterest Image], edited by [[User:Neverending Joke|Never]]. And more others are from Pinterest, too.
'''CSS''': [[User:Neverending Joke|Never]]

'''Audio''': Yume Nikki OST - Overgrown City
'''Note''': No Mercy! {{User:Neverending Joke/Credits}} }}


[[File:Land's Avoidance 4.png|center|300px]]

When morning rose, he steadied himself; eyes were fixed on the horizon, breath was measured. The fog had weakened enough to reveal shape and distance. This was the hour the stories always mentioned. If the whispers were true, then perhaps… today was the day he might find the '''Safe Place''', that hidden refuge spoken of in rumor, in faded journals, and in murmured hallucinations from the individuals who had once seen it.

[[File:Land's Avoidance 6.png|center|300px]]

In the dawnlight, a thing emerged across the water: a mountain, it was seen from a close distance but visible. Carved into its base, he saw a hollow. And then it struck him: this was not an ordinary cave. This was the one thing the rumors all agreed on: '''The Entrance to the Safe Place'''.

[[File:Land's Avoidance 8.png|center|300px]]

He stepped inside.

The cave's roughness faded away. A tunnel unfolded in its place, glowing gently, with soft warmth and deeper silence. He moved forward, and the tunnel welcomed him.

[[File:Land's Avoidance 9.png|center|300px]]

But then, it start gradually tightening.

This part had not been in any story. Trees began to rise out of the path, long and fast, their growth was unnaturally fast, as if time was moving faster here.

And far ahead: a light. Bright. Constant.

He covered his eyes and ran toward it.

[[File:Land's Avoidance 7.png|center|300px]]

He had entered the '''Safe Place''', but it did not look like what he expected and heard; instead, it was a flat expanse of ice. The ice floor was warm and dry. Beneath the icy ground, there was a dark abyss.

He walked through it.
Walked through it.

Minutes passed.
Hours passed.
And almost a day passed.

The sky was still at dawn, the ice expanse kept expanding; nothing changed and he found nothing beyond this new normality.

He checked his backpack; there was only an [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and a single chocolate bar left. [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Aquatic]] [[Category:Sky]]
